body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Worldbuilding is a competitive area, but we should differentiate by embedding just-enough context in the drafting surface (linked entities in-scene) instead of forcing writers into a separate encyclopedia workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- Campfire is strongly positioned around deep worldbuilding modules, proving demand and setting a benchmark for entity modeling: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Living Writer emphasizes cleaner drafting and structure flow, which supports our strategy to keep worldbuilding contextual and lightweight: https://livingwriter.com/features
- Inkitt’s strength is community/publishing rather than deep in-editor lore tooling, so this can be a clear product distinction for serious fiction drafting: https://writer.inkitt.com

## Risks / trade-offs
- Scope creep into full wiki/map systems can derail MVP.
- Side panel clutter can interrupt writing flow.
- Entity schema decisions now may constrain future expansion.

## Recommendation
Approve. Keep strict MVP boundaries (character/location/lore only) and require Design to define collapse behavior and glanceable in-scene context so writing remains primary.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
